scene_description: Casual home office or kitchen background. Presenter holds phone up to camera showing a viral tweet screenshot — text reads something like: 'Just found out my junior colleague uses AI to do in 10 minutes what takes me 3 hours. I've been in this industry for 15 years.' Presenter shakes head slowly with a half-smirk before speaking.
narrative_arc: The video opens with a relatable viral tweet that exposes a silent competency gap spreading across every profession, making the viewer feel the urgency of being left behind by peers — even younger, less-experienced ones. The presenter validates the feeling, then pivots to Coursiv as the fast, accessible fix — 15 minutes a day, no tech background needed, real AI skills in 28 days. It closes with a direct, low-friction CTA that frames the $20 investment as a no-brainer compared to the cost of staying stuck.
key_moment: Presenter looks straight into camera and says: 'This person has 15 years of experience and a 25-year-old with a $20 course is outrunning them. That's not a tech problem — that's a habits problem. And it's fixable in about 15 minutes a day.'
cta_script: The certificate is $20. The program is 28 days. Fifteen minutes a day. If you don't do it now, someone at your company already is — link's below.
suggested_script_outline: [0-3s HOOK] Presenter on camera, holds up phone showing viral tweet screenshot. Deadpan delivery: 'If you're still doing everything manually, you're already behind.' [3-15s PROBLEM] Presenter reads tweet aloud: 'My junior colleague uses AI to do in 10 minutes what takes me 3 hours — I've been here 15 years.' Reacts: 'I've seen this tweet shared by lawyers, marketing directors, project managers, HR leads — people with serious careers. And the honest truth? The gap is real, it's happening right now, and most people don't even know what they're falling behind on.' [15-25s SOLUTION] 'That's exactly why I did Coursiv's 28-day AI Certificate. It's built for people who are already busy — no coding, no tech background, just 15 minutes a day. I learned how to automate the stuff that was eating my mornings, write better in half the time, and actually use AI as a real work tool — not just a gimmick. Got the certificate in under a month.' [25-30s CTA] Direct eye contact, calm confidence: 'The certificate is $20. The program is 28 days. Fifteen minutes a day. If you don't do it now, someone at your company already is — link's below.'
what_to_test: A/B test the opening tweet screenshot — Version A uses a generic professional's tweet about being outpaced by AI; Version B uses an occupation-specific tweet (e.g., a marketer, lawyer, or HR manager) to measure whether niche relevance increases watch-through rate and CTR among targeted segments.
competitor_context: Coursera and LinkedIn Learning run similar 'skills gap' angles but position courses as multi-month, high-effort credentials aimed at career changers — Coursiv's edge is the 15-min/day format and $20 price point, which removes the two biggest objections (time and cost) that make those ads easy to scroll past.
risk: The tweet reaction format relies heavily on the presenter's natural credibility and delivery — if the talent feels scripted or the tweet screenshot looks staged, the UGC authenticity collapses and the borrowed social proof backfires, making the ad feel like manufactured FOMO rather than a genuine peer warning.
